paper.title: A Game of Thrones — Chapter 6 (CATELYN)
paper.author: George R. R. Martin
paper.reader_mode: spoiler-free close reading; English personal names

blocks.original_text: Of all the rooms in Winterfell’s Great Keep, Catelyn’s bedchambers were the hottest. She seldom had to light a fire. The castle had been built over natural hot springs, and the scalding waters rushed through its walls and chambers like blood through a man’s body, driving the chill from the stone halls, filling the glass gardens with a moist warmth, keeping the earth from freezing. Open pools smoked day and night in a dozen small courtyards. That was a little thing, in summer; in winter, it was the difference between life and death.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Catelyn的bedchamber是Winterfell主堡中最温暖的房间，她很少需要生火，石墙内的温泉管道提供热量。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 温泉管道让私人房间成为南方般温暖的避难所，后续政治消息不断把寒冷重新带进来。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Winterfell建在温泉之上，热水通过墙内管道为部分房间供暖。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn’s bath was always hot and steaming, and her walls warm to the touch. The warmth reminded her of Riverrun, of days in the sun with Lysa and Edmure, but Ned could never abide the heat. The Starks were made for the cold, he would tell her, and she would laugh and tell him in that case they had certainly built their castle in the wrong place.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 持续温暖让她想起Riverrun的阳光；即使多年生活在北方，她仍会借身体感觉返回故乡。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.original_text: So when they had finished, Ned rolled off and climbed from her bed, as he had a thousand times before. He crossed the room, pulled back the heavy tapestries, and threw open the high narrow windows one by one, letting the night air into the chamber.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 夫妻亲密结束后，Ned像往常一样起身开窗，让刺骨夜风进入温暖房间。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 亲密结束后立刻进入政治争论，说明Hand任命无法留在公共空间，已经进入婚姻核心。

blocks.original_text: The wind swirled around him as he stood facing the dark, naked and empty-handed. Catelyn pulled the furs to her chin and watched him. He looked somehow smaller and more vulnerable, like the youth she had wed in the sept at Riverrun, fifteen long years gone. Her loins still ached from the urgency of his lovemaking. It was a good ache. She could feel his seed within her. She prayed that it might quicken there. It had been three years since Rickon. She was not too old. She could give him another son.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ned赤身站在黑暗与寒风前，Catelyn裹紧毛皮看着他；两人对冷热的不同需求形成性格对照。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.original_text: “My duties are here in the north. I have no wish to be Robert’s Hand.”

blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.original_text: “He will not understand that. He is a king now, and kings are not like other men. If you refuse to serve him, he will wonder why, and sooner or later he will begin to suspect that you oppose him. Can’t you see the danger that would put us in?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Catelyn警告国王不是普通朋友；拒绝可能被理解为轻蔑，连婚约等“荣誉”也可能变成危险。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Catelyn把`friend`与`king`分开：私人情感不能自动约束拥有制度权力的人。

blocks.original_text: Ned shook his head, refusing to believe. “Robert would never harm me or any of mine. We were closer than brothers. He loves me. If I refuse him, he will roar and curse and bluster, and in a week we will laugh about it together. I know the man!”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ned不相信Robert会伤害自己家人，依赖两人过去比兄弟更亲密的关系。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.original_text: “You knew the man,” she said. “The king is a stranger to you.” Catelyn remembered the direwolf dead in the snow, the broken antler lodged deep in her throat. She had to make him see. “Pride is everything to a king, my lord. Robert came all this way to see you, to bring you these great honors, you cannot throw them back in his face.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Catelyn区分过去的男人与现在的国王，并想到direwolf和鹿角，认为Ned低估了改变。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `You knew the man. The king is a stranger`概括本章争论，也回应Chapter 4中Ned几乎认不出Robert。

blocks.original_text: “And in mine,” she blazed, angry now. Why couldn’t he see? “He offers his own son in marriage to our daughter, what else would you call that? Sansa might someday be queen. Her sons could rule from the Wall to the mountains of Dorne. What is so wrong with that?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Catelyn愤怒承认：国王让继承人与Sansa联姻、让Ned代行王权，是家族难得的提升。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Catelyn并非单纯追逐地位；她把王室“荣誉”理解为家族安全与无法轻易拒绝的政治现实。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Hand任命和Joffrey–Sansa婚约会显著提高House Stark在王国政治中的位置。

blocks.original_text: “Gods, Catelyn, Sansa is only eleven,” Ned said. “And Joffrey . . . Joffrey is . . . ”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ned再次指出Sansa只有十一岁，并对Joffrey明显有所保留。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.original_text: She finished for him. “ . . . crown prince, and heir to the Iron Throne. And I was only twelve when my father promised me to your brother Brandon.”

blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.original_text: That brought a bitter twist to Ned’s mouth. “Brandon. Yes. Brandon would know what to do. He always did. It was all meant for Brandon. You, Winterfell, everything. He was born to be a King’s Hand and a father to queens. I never asked for this cup to pass to me.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Brandon的名字让Ned苦涩，觉得死去的兄长更适合处理权力和荣耀。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在温暖bedchamber中展开Hand争论，Catelyn强调政治风险与家族机会，Ned强调北方职责与私人不愿。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn softened then, to see his pain. Eddard Stark had married her in Brandon’s place, as custom decreed, but the shadow of his dead brother still lay between them, as did the other, the shadow of the woman he would not name, the woman who had borne him his bastard son.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Catelyn因看见丈夫痛苦而软化；她回想自己原本要嫁Brandon，却依习俗嫁给Ned并逐渐建立爱情。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 婚姻起于替代已故兄长，却在多年共同生活中变成真实爱情；这让即将分离更有重量。

blocks.original_text: The maester was a small grey man. His eyes were grey, and quick, and saw much. His hair was grey, what little the years had left him. His robe was grey wool, trimmed with white fur, the Stark colors. Its great floppy sleeves had pockets hidden inside. Luwin was always tucking things into those sleeves and producing other things from them: books, messages, strange artifacts, toys for the children. With all he kept hidden in his sleeves, Catelyn was surprised that Maester Luwin could lift his arms at all.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Luwin身材矮小、通身灰色，目光敏锐；maester锁链由不同金属环组成，象征所学领域。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Luwin的灰色外貌与多金属锁链并置：个人低调，知识范围却广。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** maester锁链由不同金属环组成，每种金属对应其研究和掌握的领域。

blocks.original_text: The maester waited until the door had closed behind him before he spoke. “My lord,” he said to Ned, “pardon for disturbing your rest. I have been left a message.”

blocks.original_text: Many men fathered bastards. Catelyn had grown up with that knowledge. It came as no surprise to her, in the first year of her marriage, to learn that Ned had fathered a child on some girl chance met on campaign. He had a man’s needs, after all, and they had spent that year apart, Ned off at war in the south while she remained safe in her father’s castle at Riverrun. Her thoughts were more of Robb, the infant at her breast, than of the husband she scarcely knew. He was welcome to whatever solace he might find between battles. And if his seed quickened, she expected he would see to the child’s needs.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Catelyn知道许多贵族有bastards，甚至为他们提供生活；真正刺痛她的是Ned把Jon带回婚姻家庭。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Catelyn段落坦率承认她能容忍bastard存在，却不能容忍Jon被放进自己的家庭中心。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** 贵族可能承认并供养bastards，但把私生子带入婚生家庭共同抚养并不常见。

blocks.original_text: He did more than that. The Starks were not like other men. Ned brought his bastard home with him, and called him “son” for all the north to see. When the wars were over at last, and Catelyn rode to Winterfell, Jon and his wet nurse had already taken up residence.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ned不仅承认Jon，还让他与婚生子女同桌、同训；这使Catelyn每天都面对丈夫不忠的证据。

blocks.original_text: That cut deep. Ned would not speak of the mother, not so much as a word, but a castle has no secrets, and Catelyn heard her maids repeating tales they heard from the lips of her husband’s soldiers. They whispered of Ser Arthur Dayne, the Sword of the Morning, deadliest of the seven knights of Aerys’s Kingsguard, and of how their young lord had slain him in single combat. And they told how afterward Ned had carried Ser Arthur’s sword back to the beautiful young sister who awaited him in a castle called Starfall on the shores of the Summer Sea. The Lady Ashara Dayne, tall and fair, with haunting violet eyes. It had taken her a fortnight to marshal her courage, but finally, in bed one night, Catelyn had asked her husband the truth of it, asked him to his face.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 更深伤口来自Ned拒绝透露Jon母亲身份；城堡流言说她可能是Ashara Dayne。

blocks.original_text: That was the only time in all their years that Ned had ever frightened her. “Never ask me about Jon,” he said, cold as ice. “He is my blood, and that is all you need to know. blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Catelyn唯一一次追问Ashara时，Ned罕见地吓到她，命令她永远不要再问Jon。

She might have overlooked a dozen bastards for Ned’s sake, so long as they were out of sight. Jon was never out of sight, and as he grew, he looked more like Ned than any of the trueborn sons she bore him. Somehow that made it worse. “Jon must go,” she said now.
